channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Plaintiffs' lawyers call actor Gérard Depardieu a sexual predator and a misogynist as trial closes
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The plaintiffs' lawyers called French actor Gérard Depardieu a sexual predator and a “misogynist” Thursday in their final plea at a Paris trial over accusations that he sexually assaulted two women who were working on a film with him.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anti-abortion groups mount effort to strip Planned Parenthood funding ahead of Supreme Court hearing
item.description: Major anti-abortion groups are gathering in the nation's capital to begin a lobbying effort with Congress and President Donald Trump’s administration aimed at eliminating funding for Planned Parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: Planned trips this week to Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, and a dogsledding race were canceled in favor of a more limited trip to visit American military installations on the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Which fast-food burger reigns supreme? A recent survey reveals consumers' favorite one
item.description: Fast-food fans have spoken and have determined that In-N-Out Burger has the best fast-food burger, according to a recent Delish article. The food publication asked fans on social media which fast-food burger reigns supreme, with many claiming In-N-Out takes the top spot.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: The AP and the Trump administration are due back in court in their fight over White House access
item.description: The Associated Press and Trump administration are due back in court in their fight over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICE arrestees claim they were targeted for protest involvement
item.description: Students who acknowledge they participated in pro-Palestinian protests are being targeted by ICE, a complaint says
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: The Social Security Administration said Wednesday that people applying for Social Security Disability Insurance, Medicare, or Supplemental Security Income who are not able to use agency’s online portal, can complete their claim entirely over the phone instead of at an office.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Selecting the famed amphibian as commencement speaker, the school said, "honors the long history" between the university and Jim Henson.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: House Republican: White House ‘in denial’ about Signal war plans chat
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said the White House is “in denial” over The Atlantic’s explosive report about a text chain with senior-level administration officials — and, inadvertently, a reporter — that discussed plans for a U.S. attack on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Body of missing North Carolina 7-year-old recovered from river, sheriff says
item.description: "Though he is no longer with us, we know his spirit will live on in the hearts of so many forever," White said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Baron James, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department restores IDR student loan applications
item.description: The applications were put on pause after a federal appeals court ruled the Biden administration’s Saving on Valuable Education (SAVE) income-drive repayment (IDR) plan was illegal.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kristi Noem visiting El Salvador to tour Terrorist Confinement Center
item.description: Noem's slated to meet with El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele, who the administration has touted as a great partner.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Babysitter checking for monsters finds man hiding under child's bed in Kansas: Sheriff
item.description: The child reportedly told the babysitter there were monsters under the bed, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Street dog rescue saves more than 1,000 Texas animals
item.description: "I literally cry with every single dog, because I watch these dogs that have been so abused and so neglected, and they learn to be part of a family," Sasha Aghili said.
item.dc:creator: Avery Travis, NewsNation

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa death: Footage from outside home released
item.description: The footage suggests that one of the top priorities for responding deputies was the couple's privacy.
item.dc:creator: Annalisa Pardo, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Perpetual adoration is a growing Catholic trend. A Holy Year event is scheduled for the weekend
item.description: HIALEAH, Fla. (AP) — Twice a week, Luisa Arguello and her husband spend from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. praying in the perpetual adoration chapel at St. Benedict Catholic Church, tucked on a quiet palm-fringed residential street in the Miami suburb of Hialeah. “Your body clock gets used to it.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family members of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a group trip to Nashville, Tennessee, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump pardons former Hunter Biden business partner Devon Archer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned a former business partner of Hunter Biden who was convicted of participating in a conspiracy to defraud a Native American tribe.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he knows nothing of Atlantic journalist swept up in war plan text chain
item.description: President Trump said Monday he knew nothing of a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was swept up in a text message chain with defense and national security officials while they detailed plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
